CurrentBenchmarks.BenchmarkTitle: Application Security and Development Security Technical Implementation Guide

PreviousBenchmarks.BenchmarkTitle: Application Security and Development Security Technical Implementation Guide

ChangedRuleIds.RuleTitle: The application must associate organization-defined types of security attributes having organization-defined security attribute values with information in storage.
The application must associate organization-defined types of security attributes having organization-defined security attribute values with information in process.
The application must associate organization-defined types of security attributes having organization-defined security attribute values with information in transmission.
The application must enforce approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources in accordance with applicable access control policies.
The application must enforce approved authorizations for controlling the flow of information within the system based on organization-defined information flow control policies.
The application must enforce approved authorizations for controlling the flow of information between interconnected systems based on organization-defined information flow control policies.
The application must utilize FIPS-validated cryptographic modules when signing application components.
The application must utilize FIPS-validated cryptographic modules when generating cryptographic hashes.
The application must utilize FIPS-validated cryptographic modules when protecting unclassified information that requires cryptographic protection.
Applications making SAML assertions must use FIPS-approved random numbers in the generation of SessionIndex in the SAML element AuthnStatement.
The application user interface must be either physically or logically separated from data storage and management interfaces.
The application must protect the confidentiality and integrity of stored information when required by DOD policy or the information owner.
The application must maintain a separate execution domain for each executing process.
Applications must prevent unauthorized and unintended information transfer via shared system resources.

The application must implement cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure of information and/or detect changes to information during transmission unless otherwise protected by alternative physical safeguards, such as, at a minimum, a Protected Distribution System (PDS).
Security-relevant software updates and patches must be kept up to date.

The ISSO must ensure application audit trails are retained for at least 30 months (12 months active + 18 months cold storage) for applications without SAMI data and five years for applications including SAMI data.
The application must have the capability to mark sensitive/classified output when required.

The application must implement NSA-approved cryptography to protect classified information in accordance with applicable federal laws, Executive Orders, directives, policies, regulations, and standards.

CurrentBenchmarks.BenchmarkTitle: Cloud Computing Mission Owner Network Security Requirements Guide
Cloud Computing Mission Owner Operating System Security Requirements Guide

PreviousBenchmarks.BenchmarkTitle: Cloud Computing Mission Owner Network Security Requirements Guide
Cloud Computing Mission Owner Operating System Security Requirements Guide

ChildBenchmarks.BenchmarkTitle: Cloud Computing Mission Owner Network Security Requirements Guide
Cloud Computing Mission Owner Operating System Security Requirements Guide

ChangedRuleIds.RuleTitle: Least privilege access and need-to-know must be required to access the container platform registry.
Least privilege access and need-to-know must be required to access the container platform runtime.
Least privilege access and need-to-know must be required to access the container platform keystore.
The container platform must enforce approved authorizations for controlling the flow of information within the container platform based on organization-defined information flow control policies.

The container platform must enforce approved authorizations for controlling the flow of information between interconnected systems and services based on organization-defined information flow control policies.
The container platform must separate user functionality (including user interface services) from information system management functionality.
The container platform must prohibit containers from accessing privileged resources.
The container platform must prevent unauthorized and unintended information transfer via shared system resources.
The container platform runtime must maintain separate execution domains for each container by assigning each container a separate address space.

The container platform registry must contain the latest images with most recent security-relevant software updates within 30 days unless the time period is directed by an authoritative source (e.g., IAVM, CTOs, DTMs, STIGs).
The container platform runtime must have security-relevant software updates installed within 30 days unless the time period is directed by an authoritative source (e.g., IAVM, CTOs, DTMs, and STIGs).
The container platform must use a valid FIPS 140-2 or FIPS 140-3 approved cryptographic module to generate hashes.
The container platform must use a FIPS-validated cryptographic module to implement encryption services for unclassified information requiring confidentiality.

AddedGroups.RuleTitle: The container platform must be a version supported by the vendor.

CurrentBenchmarks.BenchmarkTitle: Intrusion Detection and Prevention Systems Security Requirements Guide

PreviousBenchmarks.BenchmarkTitle: Intrusion Detection and Prevention Systems Security Requirements Guide

ChangedRuleIds.RuleTitle: The IPS must enforce approved authorizations by restricting or blocking the flow of harmful or suspicious communications traffic within the network.

The IPS must restrict or block harmful or suspicious communications traffic between interconnected networks based on attribute- and content-based inspection of the source, destination, headers, and/or content of the communications traffic.
The IDPS must immediately use updates made to policy filters, rules, signatures, and anomaly analysis algorithms for traffic detection and prevention functions.

The IPS must block outbound traffic containing known and unknown denial-of-service (DoS) attacks by ensuring that security policies, signatures, rules, and anomaly detection techniques are applied to outbound communications traffic.
The IPS must block any prohibited mobile code at the enclave boundary when it is detected.
The IPS must block malicious code.
The IPS must quarantine or block malicious code.
The IPS must block outbound Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) Destination Unreachable, Redirect, and Address Mask reply messages.
The IPS must block malicious Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) packets by properly configuring ICMP signatures and rules.

To protect against unauthorized data mining, the IPS must prevent code injection attacks launched against data storage objects, including, at a minimum, databases, database records, queries, and fields.
To protect against unauthorized data mining, the IPS must prevent code injection attacks launched against application objects including, at a minimum, application URLs and application code.

To protect against unauthorized data mining, the IPS must prevent SQL injection attacks launched against data storage objects, including, at a minimum, databases, database records, and database fields.
The IPS must protect against or limit the effects of known and unknown types of denial-of-service (DoS) attacks by employing rate-based attack prevention behavior analysis.
The IPS must protect against or limit the effects of known and unknown types of denial-of-service (DoS) attacks by employing anomaly-based attack detection.
The IPS must protect against or limit the effects of known types of denial-of-service (DoS) attacks by employing signatures.

The IDPS must send an alert to, at a minimum, the information system security manager (ISSM) and information system security officer (ISSO) when intrusion detection events are detected which indicate a compromise or potential for compromise.
The IDPS must implement physically or logically separate subnetworks to isolate organization-defined critical system components and functions.

AddedGroups.RuleTitle: The IDPS must use organization-defined security attributes associated with organization-defined information, source, and destination objects to enforce organization-defined information flow control policies as a basis for flow control decisions.
The IDPS must provide visibility into network traffic at external and key internal system interfaces to optimize the effectiveness of monitoring devices.

CurrentBenchmarks.BenchmarkTitle: Microsoft Azure SQL Database Security Technical Implementation Guide

PreviousBenchmarks.BenchmarkTitle: Microsoft Azure SQL Database Security Technical Implementation Guide

ChangedRuleIds.RuleTitle: Azure SQL Databases must integrate with Azure Active Directory for providing account management and automation for all users, groups, roles, and any other principals.
Azure SQL Database must enforce approved authorizations for logical access to server information and system resources in accordance with applicable access control policies.
Azure SQL Database must enforce approved authorizations for logical access to database information and system resources in accordance with applicable access control policies.
The Azure SQL Database and associated applications must reserve the use of dynamic code execution for situations that require it.
The Azure SQL Database and associated applications, when making use of dynamic code execution, must scan input data for invalid values that may indicate a code injection attack.

The Azure SQL Database must be configured to prohibit or restrict the use of organization-defined functions, ports, protocols, and/or services, as defined in the PPSM CAL and vulnerability assessments.
Azure SQL Database must uniquely identify and authenticate organizational users (or processes acting on behalf of organizational users).
Azure SQL Database must automatically terminate a user session after organization-defined conditions or trigger events requiring session disconnect.

CurrentBenchmarks.BenchmarkTitle: Microsoft Defender Antivirus Security Technical Implementation Guide

PreviousBenchmarks.BenchmarkTitle: Microsoft Defender Antivirus Security Technical Implementation Guide

ChangedRuleIds.RuleTitle: Microsoft Defender AV must be configured to run and scan for malware and other potentially unwanted software.
Microsoft Defender AV must be configured to not exclude files for scanning.
Microsoft Defender AV must be configured to not exclude files opened by specified processes.
Microsoft Defender AV must be configured to enable the Automatic Exclusions feature.
Microsoft Defender AV must be configured to check in real time with MAPS before content is run or accessed.
Microsoft Defender AV must be configured for protocol recognition for network protection.
Microsoft Defender AV Group Policy settings must take priority over the local preference settings.
Microsoft Defender AV must monitor for incoming and outgoing files.
Microsoft Defender AV must be configured to monitor for file and program activity.
Microsoft Defender AV must be configured to always enable real-time protection.
Microsoft Defender AV must be configured to process scanning when real-time protection is enabled.
Microsoft Defender AV must be configured to scan archive files.
Microsoft Defender AV spyware definition age must not exceed 7 days.
Microsoft Defender AV virus definition age must not exceed 7 days.
Microsoft Defender AV must block Adobe Reader from creating child processes.
Microsoft Defender AV must block credential stealing from the Windows local security authority subsystem.
Microsoft Defender AV must block untrusted and unsigned processes that run from USB.
Microsoft Defender AV must use advanced protection against ransomware.
Microsoft Defender AV must audit process creations originating from PSExec and WMI commands.
Microsoft Defender AV must audit persistence through WMI event subscription.
Microsoft Defender AV must audit executable files from running unless they meet a prevalence, age, or trusted list criterion.
Microsoft Defender AV must block Office communication application from creating child processes.
Microsoft Defender AV must block abuse of exploited vulnerable signed drivers.
Microsoft Defender AV must configure local administrator merge behavior for lists.
Microsoft Defender AV must control whether exclusions are visible to Local Admins.
Microsoft Defender AV must randomize scheduled task times.
Microsoft Defender AV must hide the Family options area.
Microsoft Defender AV must enable the file hash computation feature.
Microsoft Defender AV must enable extended cloud check.
Microsoft Defender AV must enable script scanning.
Microsoft Defender AV must enable real-time protection and Security Intelligence Updates during OOBE.
Microsoft Defender AV must enable network protection to be configured into block or audit mode on Windows Server.
Microsoft Defender AV must enable EDR in block mode.
Microsoft Defender AV must report Dynamic Signature dropped events.
Microsoft Defender AV must scan excluded files and directories during quick scans.
Microsoft Defender AV must convert warn verdict to block.
Microsoft Defender AV must enable asynchronous inspection.
Microsoft Defender AV must scan packed executables.
Microsoft Defender AV must enable heuristics.
Microsoft Defender AV must set cloud protection level to High.

CurrentBenchmarks.BenchmarkTitle: Microsoft DotNet Framework 4.0 Security Technical Implementation Guide

PreviousBenchmarks.BenchmarkTitle: Microsoft DotNet Framework 4.0 Security Technical Implementation Guide

ChangedRuleIds.RuleTitle: .Net Framework versions installed on the system must be supported.
The .NET CLR must be configured to use FIPS approved encryption modules.
Trust must be established prior to enabling the loading of remote code in .Net 4.
.NET default proxy settings must be reviewed and approved.
Software utilizing .Net 4.0 must be identified and relevant access controls configured.

CurrentBenchmarks.BenchmarkTitle: Microsoft Edge Security Technical Implementation Guide

PreviousBenchmarks.BenchmarkTitle: Microsoft Edge Security Technical Implementation Guide

AddedGroups.RuleTitle: Spell checking provided by Microsoft Editor must be disabled.

ChangedRuleIds.RuleTitle: The version of Internet Explorer running on the system must be a supported version.

CurrentBenchmarks.BenchmarkTitle: Microsoft Windows 11 Security Technical Implementation Guide

PreviousBenchmarks.BenchmarkTitle: Microsoft Windows 11 Security Technical Implementation Guide

ChangedRuleIds.RuleTitle: Windows 11 systems must use a BitLocker PIN for pre-boot authentication.
The Windows 11 system must use an antivirus program.
The security event log size must be configured to a value that holds at least one week's worth of audit records.

RemovedGroups.RuleTitle: Windows 11 must employ automated mechanisms to determine the state of system components with regard to flaw remediation using the following frequency: Continuously, where ESS is used; 30 days, for any additional internal network scans not covered by ESS; and annually, for external scans by Computer Network Defense Service Provider (CNDSP).
